original_description: Cognitive self-checks at decision points — bias interrupts, reasoning-quality gates, and strategic frameworks. Invoke with a check name as argument (e.g., "wysiati", "overconfidence", "artifact-challenge", "decompose"). Use when CLAUDE.md's Cognitive Guardrails section fires, when you catch yourself rationalizing, when strategic-looping or executing-plans hits a gate, or when any skill's [eval:] tag references a guardrail. 15 checks in two families — 11 bias interrupts (evidence gaps, substitution, overconfidence, reframing, source diversity, compound probability, sunk costs, success criteria, friction decisions, learning transfer, vague self-grading) and 4 strategic frameworks (artifact-challenge, decompose, invert, leverage).
best_description: Cognitive self-checks at decision points — bias interrupts, reasoning-quality gates, and strategic frameworks. Invoke with a check name as argument (e.g., "wysiati", "overconfidence", "artifact-challenge", "decompose"). Use when CLAUDE.md's Cognitive Guardrails section fires, when you catch yourself rationalizing, when strategic-looping or executing-plans hits a gate, or when any skill's [eval:] tag references a guardrail. 15 checks in two families — 11 bias interrupts (evidence gaps, substitution, overconfidence, reframing, source diversity, compound probability, sunk costs, success criteria, friction decisions, learning transfer, vague self-grading) and 4 strategic frameworks (artifact-challenge, decompose, invert, leverage).
final_description:
Use when the user explicitly names a guardrail check as a command or instruction: "invert this", "run wysiati", "decompose check", "leverage check", "overconfidence check", "pivot-or-persist", or any of the 15 named checks invoked as a directive. Also trigger when CLAUDE.md's Cognitive Guardrails section fires, when a skill gate references a guardrail, or when catching yourself rationalizing mid-task.

The trigger test: is a named check being invoked as an instruction to apply to reasoning? Or is the user asking a substantive domain question whose vocabulary happens to overlap with a check's theme? Only trigger for the former. "Invert this: what guarantees failure?" = check invocation. "What are the real constraints here?" = domain question, not a check invocation.

history.description: Cognitive self-checks at decision points — bias interrupts, reasoning-quality gates, and strategic frameworks. Invoke with a check name as argument (e.g., "wysiati", "overconfidence", "artifact-challenge", "decompose"). Use when CLAUDE.md's Cognitive Guardrails section fires, when you catch yourself rationalizing, when strategic-looping or executing-plans hits a gate, or when any skill's [eval:] tag references a guardrail. 15 checks in two families — 11 bias interrupts (evidence gaps, substitution, overconfidence, reframing, source diversity, compound probability, sunk costs, success criteria, friction decisions, learning transfer, vague self-grading) and 4 strategic frameworks (artifact-challenge, decompose, invert, leverage).

train_results.query: we're 3 stages into executing-plans and something feels off, do a pivot-or-persist check
run the wysiati check before I finalize this migration plan
overconfidence check: I'm about to claim the race condition is definitely in the scheduler
I've only looked at the error logs and our own codebase — availability bias check
I keep second-guessing this architecture decision, can you do a reframe check
leverage check: which single change to the pipeline scheduler would make everything else easier
we have 4 independent refactor tasks, dispatch them in parallel with subagents
run the brainstorming skill before we design the new notification system
I've been heads-down on this for 2 hours and want to make sure I'm still solving the right problem — check the strategic-looping skill
generate characterization tests for payments.py before I touch it — I want a safety net
I need to stress test the /payments endpoint for race conditions and edge cases
before I keep going on this plan, what are the real constraints vs ones we inherited from the old API design
invert this: what would guarantee the caching layer fails under load

test_results.query: decompose the constraints here — I suspect we inherited assumptions from the old system that don't apply
I think I'm rationalizing keeping the old auth system — run sunk-cost check
before we ship this, hit the artifact-challenge guardrail on the design doc
I said the refactor is 'good quality' but that's vague — operationalize that before we close the ticket
evaluate whether my agent picked the right tool at the phase transition — use eval-protocol
I'm not sure if this approach is right, can you check the mulch knowledge base for prior decisions on our caching strategy
do a full codebase diagnostic on the auth module, I think the architecture is wrong

history.description: Use for metacognitive self-checks on Claude's own reasoning quality — bias interrupts and strategic frameworks applied to Claude's decision-making process. Trigger when explicitly naming a check ("run wysiati", "overconfidence check", "invert this", "leverage check", "do a decompose"), when CLAUDE.md's Cognitive Guardrails section fires, when a skill gate calls for a guardrail, or when catching yourself rationalizing a decision mid-task. The 15 checks cover: evidence gaps (wysiati), substitution, overconfidence, reframing, source diversity, compound probability, sunk costs, success criteria, friction decisions, learning transfer, vague self-grading, plus artifact-challenge, decompose, invert, and leverage. Do NOT trigger when the user is asking a substantive domain question about constraints, architecture, or design — only trigger when the request is for Claude to apply a reasoning self-check to its own thinking process.
